pages.raw_text:
Alaska-Yukon- Pacific monument is
more than 60 per cent completed,
The ground work consisting of
grading and the light. gas, tele-
phone, sewer and water systems
are in and the site has been clear
ed and excavation work. finished
for the United States government
buildings. The paving is now 40
per cent completed and within the
next 80 days more than one half
of the IL miles Of streets and
walks on the exposition grounds
will be paved with asphalt.


The design for the Women's
building has been submitted and
the plans for the Spokane county
structure have been accepted. The
work of building the exposition has
progressed to such an extent that
the main entl dnce gates are now
being built. On September 20 the
exposition commenced charging an
admission of 10 cents to the
grounds and up to last Saturday
there had been more than 100000
paid admissions.
